pages.full_text: empty of water because the tide was out St. Non gave birth to St. David just there. A holy well, enormous stones in ruined chapel. St. Non reputed to be neice of Arthur? We saw a dalmation with panniers, carrying his own food + drink.
pages.notes: Page contains a large glued-in photo reproduction of a painting (likely Constable based on right-page caption). Text is a continuation from a previous page — begins mid-sentence 'empty of water because the tide was out'. St. Non is a saint associated with St. Davids, Pembrokeshire, Wales. The word 'panniers' could also be read as 'paniers'. No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner.

pages.full_text:
Salisbury Cathedral from the meadows 1831 Constable painted after his wife died. Great friendship with John Fisher-house where rainbow ends.

s out. Wind gusts up to 40 mph now.               5 saints
Saturday              Dolaucothi          Pumpsaint stone
lots of rain. [?Wnts] We walked along the cliffs + saw enormous waves crashing + cresting the cliffs. We went on [illegible] to a Gold Mine stopping at Morrisons on the way. We
photo_captions: Salisbury Cathedral from the meadows 1831 Constable painted after his wife died. Great friendship with John Fisher-house where rainbow ends.
pages.notes: Page contains a large glued-in photo of Constable's 'Salisbury Cathedral from the Meadows' (1831), with a handwritten caption in the right margin. The text at the bottom continues from and across to the left page — 's out.' at the start of the lower text is the tail end of the sentence about the tide from the left page. 'Saturday' is underlined as a heading. 'Dolaucothi' and 'Pumpsaint' are in Wales (Carmarthenshire); Dolaucothi Gold Mines is a National Trust site near Pumpsaint. The word before 'We walked' is partially obscured and reads like '[?Wnts]' or could be 'Wanta' or similar — possibly 'Wants' or a place name fragment. 'John Fisher' refers to Archdeacon John Fisher, Constable's friend in Salisbury. Text ends mid-sentence 'We' — continues on next spread. Year cannot be determined from this page alone; context (Wales/Pembrokeshire trip, St Davids area) does not confirm a specific year within 2003-2019.
